title: echioides ciliata
intro: Echioides ciliata, also known as the Toothache Plant or Polynesian Pawpaw, is a species of flowering plant in the family Echiuriaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating toothaches, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues. The medicinal properties of Echioides ciliata are attributed to its sap, which contains compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing relief from pain and reducing the risk of infection. In herbal preparations, the sap of the plant is often extracted and used to create topical creams, ointments, and mouthwashes, which can be used to alleviate oral health issues.
